items.title: Caution and long-term thinking as budget adds to softening real estate market

children.content: After riding a post-COVID wave of booming prices, real estate agents say SA's housing market is starting to level out, as federal budget tax changes and rising interest rates reduce buyer appetite.

items.title: Collingwood champion's achievement reopens old wound outside Victoria

children.content: When Scott Pendlebury steps onto the MCG on Saturday afternoon, his name will stand alone at the top of a remarkable list — but the achievement has also reignited a debate about the value of non-Victorian Aussie Rules records.

image.altText: Craig Bradley moves the ball forward for South Australia.

items.title: Fracking could open the door to 10 years of gas supply but is there a risk?

children.content: A decade's worth of gas supply for South Australia is potentially sitting below the Limestone Coast region. But will it ever be extracted?

image.altText: A farmer holds an anti mining yellow sign by a gate on a farm

items.title: Is Australia's two-party political system dead?

children.content: With the rise of One Nation's popularity and younger voters increasingly turning to the Greens, the traditional two-party system in Australian politics has been dismantled, a former political strategist argues.

gallery.altText: Composite of Greens volunteers holding placards and Pauline Hanson meeting One Nation voters

thumbnail.altText: Composite of Greens volunteers holding placards and Pauline Hanson meeting One Nation voters

items.title: Why there's more talk of an AUKUS 'plan B'

children.content: Defence industry experts argue Australia should consider how to prevent a naval "capability gap" — or risk being left exposed.

image.altText: A crowd of people standing on a submarine.

items.title: Little penguin numbers climb on Kangaroo Island despite algal bloom impact

children.content: Little penguin numbers on Kangaroo Island have rebounded, but estimates show there are only about 700 adult breeders after a harmful algal bloom.

image.altText: A little penguin peers out of a rocky burrow

items.title: The prickly plant powering a new spirits industry

children.content: Australia's emerging agave industry is attracting interest from distillers, farmers and scientists who believe the spiky plant could transform everything from tequila production to sustainable aviation fuel.

image.altText: Close up of an agave plant.

items.title: Boat driver jailed after running over swimmer who suffered 'horrific injuries'

children.content: A woman who was likely more than three times the legal blood alcohol limit when she ran over a swimmer while driving a boat has been jailed for more than two years.

image.altText: A blonde woman wearing large sunglasses and smiling

items.title: Doctors push for e-bike ban in SA for riders aged under 16

children.content: South Australia's peak medical body is calling for reforms to current e-bike laws in the state, including a ban on children aged under 16 from riding them.

image.altText: E bikes lined up in a row inside a bike shop

items.title: 36ers eye dream team as former Perth coach reunites with Bryce Cotton

children.content: One of Australian basketball's most successful coaches and one of the national league's greatest players have paired in Adelaide.

image.altText: A composite image of two men

items.title: Why consumers are forking out more for whiting, calamari and garfish

children.content: South Australia's peak seafood body says a short supply of some species due to the algal bloom and commercial fishing restrictions has led to slight price increases, as it warns it could take industry five to 10 years to fully recover.

image.altText: Fillets for sale at a fishmonger at the Adelaide Central Market.

items.title: Caravan repairers battling to stay afloat as travellers stay close to home

children.content: Normally at this time of year, RVs are hitting the road to head north and escape winter. But the ongoing fuel crisis is forcing travellers to stay closer to home, causing a serious economic downturn for many of the 2,500 businesses in the RV repair industry.

image.altText: RVs at a caravan park

items.title: ASIO holiday warning did not flag elevated Hanukkah threat

children.content: Australia's intelligence agency, ASIO, issued its annual holiday threat assessment less than two weeks before the Hanukkah terrorist attack at Bondi Beach but did not identify heightened risks to the Jewish festival, despite earlier declaring antisemitism as its top priority in terms of threat to life.

items.title: Children's entertainer Danny Go!'s son dies of cancer aged 14

children.content: The popular children's YouTuber revealed the news after documenting the illness since it was diagnosed late last year. Tributes have flowed from Children's entertainers, including the Wiggles.

image.altText: The YouTuber shared the news of his son's diagnosis in December, last year.

items.title: Pendlebury honoured in record-breaking game, Roos beat Suns after the siren

children.content: North Melbourne comes from 43 points back to beat Gold Coast after the siren before attention turns to the MCG, where Collingwood's Scott Pendlebury will break the V/AFL games record against West Coast. Follow live.

items.title: For Willie Rioli now is time to 'give back' to the place that gave him everything

children.content: The Tiwi Islands are unique and Australian Rules football is a way of life that has spawned some of the game's biggest names. But even as favourite son Willie Rioli Jr returns home, the Tiwi Bombers know they need more help.

items.title: Death toll jumps to 90 after Chinese coal mine blast

children.content: A gas explosion at a coal mine in northern China's Shanxi province has jumped to 90, state media reports. Nine people remain missing.

items.title: 'Beautiful, kind, caring man' dies in hospital months after alleged bashing

children.content: Friends of Darwin man Martin Wiberg, 48, are remembering him as a "very gentle soul" after he died in hospital this week.

items.title: The mission to save conjoined twin involved an airlift, five surgeons and a heartbreaking sacrifice

children.content: A team of doctors involved in the separation of conjoined Papua New Guinean twins Sawong and Tom have spoken for the first time about the rare and complex case.

items.title: Brent Harvey is about to lose his place in AFL history

children.content: Brent 'Boomer' Harvey has enjoyed being the AFL/VFL games record holder for the past decade, and says he could have played more if he'd accepted an offer from other clubs to extend his career.

image.altText: Brent Harvey smiles while sitting on an armchair. He wears a black t-shirt.

items.title: Black mould and broken toilets: Inside 'dismal' government-owned homes

children.content: Bug infestations, walls covered in black mould and broken toilets are just some of the issues plaguing homes in Australia's most isolated communities, where the landlord is the state government.

image.altText: Black mould and insects cover a wall and ceiling around Christian statues.

items.title: Suzie Miller took the risky path away from a safe job as a judge, with no regrets

items.title: Psychiatrist repeatedly harassed nurse on messaging platforms

children.content: The only psychiatrist in a regional WA area is given a more lenient sanction for sexually harassing a nurse, partially due to a lack of other mental health specialists in the area.
